questions.prompt: Your team needs to extract data from receipt photos uploaded by users (merchant, date, amount, line items). What's the right OpenAI capability?

choices.text: Use a separate OCR library, then feed the OCR text to GPT-5.

Use vision input on a GPT-5 family model — pass the receipt image directly. The model handles OCR implicitly and you can combine with structured output to return typed fields. Single call, simpler pipeline.
Use image generation to recreate the receipt.

questions.explanation: Modern GPT-5 family models read text in images natively — no separate OCR step. Combine with structured output (Lesson 2.2) and you get a typed object back from a single API call. Simpler pipeline, often higher accuracy than OCR + LLM as separate steps.

questions.prompt: You're building a phone-call-style AI agent where users talk and the agent talks back. Latency matters — long pauses break the experience. Which OpenAI capability is the right tool?

choices.text: Speech-to-text → GPT-5 → text-to-speech, called sequentially. Simple.

The Realtime API — bidirectional streaming designed for sub-second voice responses including model processing. Sequential STT → LLM → TTS will introduce 2–4 second latency per turn, breaking the interactive feel.

questions.explanation: Sequential STT → LLM → TTS works for any voice product, but the latency (2–4 seconds per turn) makes it feel slow for interactive voice. The Realtime API streams audio in and out with sub-second latency including model processing. It's more complex to integrate, but worth it when latency is the product.
